title: Deploy loopctl to Fly.io with managed PostgreSQL

story.i_want_to: deploy loopctl to Fly.io with a managed PostgreSQL database and automated CI/CD
story.so_that: the service is publicly accessible at loopctl.com with production-grade reliability, automatic deployments on merge to master, and no dependency on the self-hosted beelink server

rationale: loopctl is currently self-hosted on a beelink machine behind a residential network. To offer it as a public service, it needs to run on cloud infrastructure with proper SSL, managed database, health monitoring, and automated deploys. Fly.io is the proven platform — both home_care_billing and reportex already run there successfully with the same Phoenix/Elixir stack.

acceptance_criteria.description: fly.toml is updated from placeholder to production-ready: app name 'loopctl', primary_region 'ord' (or 'lax'), PHX_HOST set to 'loopctl.com', internal_port 8080 (matching other Fly deployments), min_machines_running 1, health check on /health.

Dockerfile is updated for Fly.io deployment: port 8080 (not 4000), EXPOSE 8080, runtime deps match current Dockerfile but optimized for the Fly environment (no tini needed — Fly handles process management).

Fly Managed PostgreSQL is provisioned with RLS support: the init script (deploy/init-db.sh or equivalent) creates loopctl_app and loopctl_admin roles, enables RLS on all tables, and grants appropriate privileges. DATABASE_URL and ADMIN_DATABASE_URL are set as Fly secrets.

Runtime config (config/runtime.exs) detects Fly.io environment via FLY_APP_NAME env var and applies Fly-specific database tuning: connect_timeout, queue_target, queue_interval adjusted for PgBouncer (matching reportex patterns).
All required secrets are set via 'fly secrets set': DATABASE_URL, ADMIN_DATABASE_URL, SECRET_KEY_BASE, CLOAK_KEY. No secrets are hardcoded in fly.toml or Dockerfile.
Release command '/app/bin/migrate' runs Ecto migrations on deploy, including the RLS policy setup migration. The migration succeeds on a fresh database.
DNS is configured: loopctl.com points to the Fly.io app via CNAME or A record. Fly.io issues and manages the SSL certificate automatically. HTTPS is enforced (force_https: true).

GitHub Actions CI/CD workflow deploys to Fly.io on push to master: lint → test (with RLS-aware Postgres setup) → security (sobelow) → dialyzer → deploy. Deploy step uses 'flyctl deploy --remote-only'. Matches the pattern from home_care_billing and reportex workflows.
Session affinity is enabled if LiveView is in use (from US-18.1): sticky sessions with TTL 3600s, h2_backend disabled for WebSocket stability.

After deployment, GET https://loopctl.com returns the landing page (from US-18.1), GET https://loopctl.com/health returns {status: ok}, and GET https://loopctl.com/api/v1/ returns the API discovery JSON. All existing API endpoints are functional.
The self-hosted beelink deployment (docker-compose) continues to work for local/development use. Fly.io deployment does not break the existing docker-compose setup.

test_cases.name: Health check endpoint returns ok on Fly.io
expected_results: Returns 200 with JSON {status: ok, database: connected}

test_cases.name: API routes are accessible on Fly.io
expected_results: /api/v1/projects returns 200 with projects list (or empty array)

test_cases.name: RLS policies work correctly on Fly Managed Postgres
preconditions: Two tenants with API keys exist
steps: Create a project under tenant A via API
Attempt to list projects as tenant B
expected_results: Tenant B cannot see tenant A's project
RLS policies are enforced at the database level

test_cases.name: Migrations run successfully on deploy
preconditions: Fresh Fly.io deployment with new database
steps: Check release command logs for migration output
expected_results: RLS policies are created by the migration
Both loopctl_app and loopctl_admin roles exist

test_cases.name: HTTPS is enforced and certificate is valid
expected_results: HTTP request redirects to HTTPS (301 or 308)
HTTPS request returns 200 with valid TLS certificate for loopctl.com

test_cases.name: CI/CD pipeline deploys on master push
preconditions: FLY_API_TOKEN is set as a GitHub secret
expected_results: Workflow runs: lint → test → security → dialyzer → deploy
New version is live at loopctl.com

technical_notes: Reference deployments: home_care_billing (fly.toml, Dockerfile, .github/workflows/ci.yml) and reportex (same files)
Fly.io provisioning: fly launch (skip if app exists), fly postgres create, fly secrets set
RLS on Fly Managed Postgres: need to run init-db.sh equivalent via fly postgres connect or as part of the migration
Fly Managed Postgres uses PgBouncer by default — use session mode for prepared statements + LISTEN/NOTIFY
Runtime config should detect FLY_APP_NAME to apply Fly-specific DB tuning (see reportex config/runtime.exs)
DNS: configure loopctl.com via fly certs add loopctl.com, then set CNAME at registrar
Port: change from 4000 to 8080 to match other Fly deployments (Fly routes external 443 → internal port)
GitHub Actions: add FLY_API_TOKEN secret, use superfly/flyctl-actions for deploy step
The beelink docker-compose deployment should remain as a fallback/dev option
Consider running 'fly scale memory 1024' if 512MB proves insufficient with LiveView + API traffic
Oban queue config: ensure Oban runs on Fly.io (webhook delivery worker needs outbound HTTP)
